_about: Frozen, hand-labeled gold standard for validating the semantic_rescorer LLM judge. Each unit is one (contract, missed ground-truth vuln) decision from the Opus 4.8 run (results_real__claude-opus-4-8.json). gold=true means a security auditor would agree the model's UNMATCHED finding pool for that contract contains a finding referring to the SAME underlying vulnerability. Labeling rule: true if a finding names the same mechanism OR a consequence that uniquely implies it; false if no finding captures the actual mechanism. Borderline calls (noted) were labeled conservatively to avoid inflating judge agreement. Labeled by repo author; intended to be audited/disputed.

labels.note: message_proof_bypass = accepting messages without a valid proof; the zero-root init is exactly what enables that bypass (same fix).
BORDERLINE: default/zero value treated as valid is the same Nomad mechanism surfaced as message_proof_bypass.
no finding addresses upgrade-time state validation.
no finding names replay; message_proof_bypass is about proof acceptance, not replay.
missing_event_and_no_op_depositETH / missing_balance_validation (zero/EOA address) directly capture the 0-value deposit being credited.
no_balance_check_on_low_level_transfer / missing_balance_validation = missing input validation on deposit.
arbitrary_delegatecall_approval_drain = approval exploitation.
unvalidated_route_registration = faulty route validation.
approval_drain / arbitrary_token_listing = approval exploitation.
arbitrary_external_call / approval_drain names it exactly.
approval_drain component covers infinite-approval drain.
no finding captures flash-loan pool-price skew; missing_slippage_protection is tangential.
BORDERLINE: missing_slippage_protection is related but is not the spot-price-dependency mechanism; labeled false (conservative).
missing_message_source_validation / weak_source_authentication = not verifying gateway origin.
weak_source_authentication = unauthenticated handler.
missing_message_source_validation is the mechanism that lets forged messages through.
BORDERLINE->false: Opus flagged a general 'trusts proof validation' concern, not the MMR bounds-check mechanism; conservative non-match.
governance_authentication_weakness + authentication_bypass_via_self_from capture the weak-governance mint-authority takeover.
authentication_bypass_via_self_from / cross_chain_replay = forged messages accepted.
the reentrancy was already a TP; no remaining finding identifies the untrusted external callee specifically.
arbitrary_external_call / approval_drain names it.
BORDERLINE: redundant with the arbitrary-call finding (unvalidated callee/calldata IS the missing input validation).
unvalidated_flashloan_callback_initiator = unvalidated callback.
missing_maxFeePercentage_and_input_validation = missing input validation.
GENUINE MISS: pool (checked_arithmetic_dependency, trusted_external_call_dependency) does not capture exchange-rate/donation manipulation.
GENUINE MISS: no finding about redeem/share rounding.
GENUINE MISS: no finding about first-depositor/donation.
approval_drain_arbitrary_call names it.
missing_validation_flashloan_callback = unvalidated callback.
missing_validation_flashloan_callback covers missing input validation.
solvency_check_bypass = missing solvency check.
state_flag_corruption = the flag reset bypassing the end-of-call check.
solvency_check_bypass + state_flag_corruption are the specific logic flaw.
forged_deposit_event / unauthenticated_memo = event spoofing.
arbitrary_external_call / approval_drain names it.
BORDERLINE: arbitrary_call_approval_drain is the exploit the improper whitelist enables; auditor would call this the same bug.
arbitrary_call_approval_drain names it.
arbitrary_call_approval_drain + infinite_approval_standing_allowance = approval exploitation.
